questions.q: Tessy Thomas is popularly known by which nickname?
questions.explanation: Tessy Thomas earned the nickname 'The Missile Woman of India' for her leading role in the country's missile programme.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas is associated with which family of Indian missiles?
questions.explanation: She is best known for directing India's long-range Agni ballistic missile programme.

questions.q: What notable 'first' is Tessy Thomas credited with in the Indian missile programme?
options: First woman Nobel laureate in physics from India
First woman to lead an Indian missile project
questions.explanation: She became the first woman scientist to head a missile project in India.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas spent the bulk of her career working for which Indian defence organisation?
questions.explanation: She is a scientist with the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO).

questions.q: In which Indian state was Tessy Thomas born?
questions.explanation: Tessy Thomas was born in Alappuzha (Alleppey), Kerala, in 1963.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas served as the Project Director for which specific Agni missile variant?
questions.explanation: She served as Project Director for Agni-IV, a key long-range variant of the Agni series.

questions.q: Agni missiles are primarily designed for which role?
questions.explanation: The Agni series consists of surface-to-surface ballistic missiles for strategic deterrence.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas's professional field is best described as which discipline?
questions.explanation: She is an aerospace scientist specialising in missile guidance and systems.

questions.q: Which legendary Indian scientist, who launched the integrated missile programme, was a key mentor figure for the Agni missile work Tessy Thomas joined?
questions.explanation: A. P. J. Abdul Kalam led the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme under which the Agni work began.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas is celebrated as a role model for which group in Indian science?
questions.explanation: As the first woman to head an Indian missile project, she is widely seen as a role model for women in STEM.

questions.q: In which year was Tessy Thomas born?
questions.explanation: Tessy Thomas was born on 6 April 1963 in Alappuzha, Kerala.

questions.q: It is often noted that Tessy Thomas was named after which historical figure?
questions.explanation: Her name is commonly said to have been inspired by Mother Teresa.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas earned her undergraduate engineering degree in which branch?
questions.explanation: She graduated in electrical engineering before specialising in guided-missile work.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas pursued a postgraduate (M.Tech) specialisation closely tied to missile work in which area?
questions.explanation: She completed an M.Tech with a specialisation in guided-missile technology from the Institute of Armament Technology, Pune.

questions.q: The Agni-IV missile, whose project Tessy Thomas directed, falls into which range category?
questions.explanation: Agni-IV is an intermediate-to-long-range ballistic missile capable of reaching several thousand kilometres.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas was associated with the development and testing of the guidance systems for Agni missiles in roughly which decade she joined the programme?
questions.explanation: She joined the DRDO missile programme in the late 1980s, early in the Agni development effort.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas later took on a senior leadership role as Director General of which DRDO cluster?
questions.explanation: She was appointed Director General of Aeronautical Systems at DRDO.

questions.q: Which agency built the Agni missiles that Tessy Thomas worked on, distinguishing them from civilian space launch vehicles?
options: A civilian space agency for satellite launches
A defence research organisation for strategic weapons
questions.explanation: Agni is a strategic weapons programme of DRDO, distinct from ISRO's civilian satellite launch vehicles.

questions.q: What is the literal meaning of 'Agni', the name of the missile series Tessy Thomas led?
questions.explanation: 'Agni' is the Sanskrit word for fire, also the name of the Hindu fire deity.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas's career is most often cited as breaking which kind of barrier in Indian defence science?
options: A gender barrier in a male-dominated field
questions.explanation: She broke a major gender barrier by leading a high-profile missile project in a traditionally male-dominated domain.

questions.q: Within missile engineering, Tessy Thomas's core technical expertise is most closely associated with which subsystem?
questions.explanation: Her expertise centres on missile guidance and navigation systems.

questions.q: The Agni programme that Tessy Thomas advanced is central to which national capability?
questions.explanation: The Agni ballistic missiles form a key part of India's strategic deterrence and delivery capability.

questions.q: From which Pune-based institution did Tessy Thomas complete her guided-missile postgraduate studies?
options: Film and Television Institute of India
questions.explanation: She studied guided missiles at the Institute of Armament Technology, Pune, now the Defence Institute of Advanced Technology (DIAT).

questions.q: Tessy Thomas is reported to have completed doctoral-level study (PhD) in a field connected to which technical area?
questions.explanation: She pursued doctoral studies tied to her missile and systems work rather than any unrelated field.

questions.q: Before directing Agni-IV, Tessy Thomas notably contributed to the propulsion/guidance design work of which earlier long-range Agni variant?
questions.explanation: She worked on the Agni-III guidance system before becoming Project Director for Agni-IV.

questions.q: The Agni-IV missile led by Tessy Thomas's team is notable for being a two-stage system using which propellant type?
questions.explanation: Agni-IV is a two-stage, solid-propellant ballistic missile.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas's leadership of a strategic missile project is frequently compared to the legacy of which mentor whom she described as a major influence?
questions.explanation: She has repeatedly credited A. P. J. Abdul Kalam, architect of India's missile programme, as her key mentor and inspiration.

questions.q: Which statement best captures why Tessy Thomas's appointment as an Agni project director was historically significant?
options: She was the first foreign-born project director
She was the first woman to lead a missile project in India
She was the youngest person ever to join DRDO
She was the first non-engineer to lead a missile project
questions.explanation: Her significance lies in being the first woman to head a missile project in India, a landmark in the country's defence science.

questions.q: The Agni missile family that Tessy Thomas advanced originated as part of which broader 1980s Indian programme?
questions.explanation: The Agni series emerged from the Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme (IGMDP) launched in the 1980s.

questions.q: Tessy Thomas's career trajectory from guidance engineer to Director General of Aeronautical Systems illustrates which broader theme in Indian science?
options: Rise of women into senior strategic technology leadership
Shift from defence to agriculture research
questions.explanation: Her rise to a Director General role exemplifies the growing presence of women in senior strategic-technology leadership in India.
